name: Maasai Mara
description: Vast rolling plains famous for the Great Migration and high density of big cats.

name: Amboseli
description: Famous for its massive elephant herds and spectacular views of Mt. Kilimanjaro.

name: Watamu Marine
description: UNESCO Biosphere Reserve featuring coral gardens and home to green turtles.

name: Kisite Mpunguti
description: Known as the 'Home of the Dolphins,' offering snorkeling over coral reefs.

name: Nairobi Museum
description: Showcases Kenya's heritage with prehistoric fossils, artifacts, and art.

name: Hell's Gate
description: Scenic park with dramatic cliffs, allowing cycling and hiking past wildlife.

name: Mt. Kenya
description: Africa's second-highest peak, featuring glaciers and moorlands for hiking.

name: Mt. Longonot
description: Extinct volcano with a thick forest inside its crater, popular for rim hiking.

name: Lake Naivasha
description: Freshwater lake known for hippos and birdlife, offering boat safaris.

name: Fort Jesus
description: UNESCO site and 16th-century fortress showcasing Swahili history.

name: Gede Ruins
description: 13th-century Swahili settlement ruins, now a national monument.

name: Malindi Marine
description: Features fringing reefs and seagrass beds, ideal for snorkeling.

name: Karura Forest
description: Urban forest with waterfalls and trails for cycling and bird watching.

name: Tsavo East
description: Kenya's largest park, famous for 'red' elephants and the Yatta Plateau.

name: Tsavo West
description: Rugged landscape with Mzima Springs, rhino sanctuary, and lava flows.

name: Lake Nakuru
description: Alkaline lake famous for seasonal flamingo flocks and rhino sanctuary.

name: Samburu
description: Arid landscape home to the 'Samburu Special Five' and rich culture.

name: Ol Pejeta
description: Conservancy hosting the last northern white rhinos and a chimp sanctuary.

name: Aberdare
description: High-altitude park with thick rainforest and spectacular waterfalls.

name: Meru
description: Untamed wilderness with jungles, birthplace of the 'Born Free' story.

name: Bomas of Kenya
description: Cultural center displaying traditional homesteads and tribal dances.

name: Kiunga Marine
description: Encompasses an archipelago with coral reefs and turtle nesting sites.

name: Mombasa Marine
description: Popular for watersports and rich marine life near coastal resorts.

name: Arabuko Sokoke
description: Largest coastal forest in East Africa, home to endemic species.

name: Shimba Hills
description: Coastal rainforest with high biodiversity and rare sable antelope.

name: Taita Hills Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Private sanctuary famous for stilt lodges and World War I history.
